---
title: "Suddenly cannot do a DNS query"
date: 2020-08-24
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by gjaffe on 2020-08-24
Two days ago, my computer (LXDE 16.04) lost the ability to do a DNS query. When I do

dig google.com

I get

; <<>> DiG 9.10.3-P4-Ubuntu <<>> google.com
;; global options: +cmd
;; connection timed out; no servers could be reached

nslookup times out, too.  My /etc/resolv.conf looks like this

# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
nameserver 127.0.1.1

I've been putting various domain names in my /etc/hosts file, but this does not scale well.  Any suggestions on how to fix this would be greatly appreciated.

Gary

---

### Post by rsteinmetz70112 on 2020-08-24
Perhaps your ISPs DNS service sucks?

---

### Post by gjaffe on 2020-08-24
Thanks, but I've tried switching to opendns, google, and others.  I can't do a DNS query on any of them.

---

### Post by rsteinmetz70112 on 2020-08-26
You could try adding a nameserver to /etc/resolv.conf for testing. it will be rewritten whne you reboot but it should work until then.
How are you setting the DNS servers?
